claim_gate.reason: One uniform orbit Hamiltonian supplies finite theorem targets for the six missing labels, but those targets still lack exact all-n spectra, normalized gaps, coherent implementation, and a decoder.

falsifiers_triggered: Finite splitting at three sizes does not imply an all-n simple spectrum.
Near-integer floating characteristic coefficients are not exact identities.
An observed raw or LCU-normalized gap is not a uniform asymptotic lower bound.
A formal orbit sum is not a compiled coherent SELECT/PREPARE circuit.
Complete intermediate labels would still not constitute a hidden-involution decoder or a classical separation.

mathematical_contract.intermediate_family: the exact nine padded shapes from the character-polynomial certificate
mathematical_contract.operator: sum rho_eta(tau) tensor rho_W(c) over transpositions tau and oriented three-cycles c with support intersection two
mathematical_contract.proof_boundary: floating finite spectra and integer polynomial reconstruction are conjecture targets only
mathematical_contract.target_extraction: one separating YJM tableau label; diagonal equivariance makes the multiplicity spectrum independent of the chosen final-irrep tableau

summary: The same support-intersection-two orbit Hamiltonian split all 21 audited nontrivial blocks across n=8..10, yielding finite integer-polynomial targets for all 6 previously unproved shape families; no new all-n or coherent label theorem follows.
